displayDescription.zh:
DBSCAN 用邻域密度而不是预设簇数定义聚类。若一个点在半径 ε 内至少拥有 MinPts 个邻居，它就是核心点；算法从核心点向密度可达的邻居扩展，将连接起来的高密度区域组成簇，并把无法归入任何簇的稀疏点标记为噪声。

这种定义可以发现弯曲或不规则形状的簇，并在聚类过程中显式处理异常点，特别适合空间数据。DBSCAN 对 ε、MinPts 和距离尺度较敏感，在不同区域密度差异很大或高维距离失去区分力时表现会下降。它仍是“以密度发现结构”的经典方法。
displayDescription.en:
DBSCAN defines clusters through neighborhood density instead of a predetermined number of groups. A point is a core point when at least MinPts neighbors lie within radius ε. The algorithm expands from core points through density-reachable neighbors, joining connected high-density regions into clusters and labeling sparse points that belong to none of them as noise.

This definition can discover curved or irregular cluster shapes and handles outliers explicitly during clustering, making it especially useful for spatial data. DBSCAN is sensitive to ε, MinPts, and feature scale, and it struggles when different regions have very different densities or when high-dimensional distances lose discrimination. It remains the classic demonstration that clusters can be defined by density rather than centroids.

title.en: A density-based algorithm for discovering clusters
title.zh: 一种发现簇的基于密度的算法

description.en: Original KDD paper introducing DBSCAN's density reachability and noise-aware clustering.
description.zh: KDD 原始论文：介绍 DBSCAN 的密度可达与噪声感知聚类。

html.zh: 许多聚类方法偏好圆形簇，并要求分析前指定簇数量。 这段背景帮助观众把DBSCAN 密度聚类放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。
html.en: Many clustering methods preferred round clusters and required the number of clusters before analysis. This context helps viewers place DBSCAN in the technical problems and research priorities of its time.

html.zh: 如果一个点在 epsilon 范围内有足够邻居，它就是核心点；簇通过密度可达链条扩展。 关键机制是基于 epsilon 与 MinPts 的密度可达，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。
html.en: A point with enough neighbors within epsilon is a core point; clusters expand through chains of density reachability. The key mechanism is Density reachability with epsilon and MinPts, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.

html.zh: 专家通常把 DBSCAN 视为标准的基于密度聚类方法，尤其适用于空间数据和不规则簇形状。它的长期影响在于核心点、边界点和噪声点的语言让异常处理和任意形状簇变得实用。
html.en: Experts generally treat DBSCAN as a standard density-based clustering method, especially for spatial data and irregular cluster shapes. Its long-term legacy is the core-border-noise vocabulary that made outlier handling and arbitrary-shape clusters practical.

notes.zh: 由 legacy generated milestone 批量生成的 variant；展示层默认不覆盖 legacy。
notes.en: Variant batch-generated from the generated legacy milestone; presentation does not override legacy by default.

name.en: Hans-Peter Kriegel, Jorg Sander, Xiaowei Xu
name.zh: 汉斯-彼得·克里格尔、约尔格·桑德、徐晓伟

quote.en: "Clusters are dense regions separated by sparse noise"
quote.zh: "簇是被稀疏噪声隔开的密集区域"

workTitle.en: A density-based algorithm for discovering clusters in large spatial databases with noise
workTitle.zh: 一种在含噪大型空间数据库中发现簇的基于密度的算法

demo.zh: 从核心点生长簇并标记噪声

text.en: A point with enough neighbors in an epsilon radius.
text.zh: epsilon 半径内邻居足够多的点。

text.en: Clusters grow by connecting dense neighborhoods.
text.zh: 簇通过连接密集邻域生长。

text.en: Sparse points can remain outside all clusters.
text.zh: 稀疏点可以不属于任何簇。
